(Temple Guardians of the Tunga — Sringeri)

In the sacred waters of the Tunga River flowing beside the Sringeri Sharada Peetham, native freshwater fish live in rare harmony with people and tradition. Species like the powerful Deccan Mahseer (Tor khudree), Malabar Mahseer (Tor Malabaricus), Hypselobarbus varieties, and resilient Catfish such as Wallago attu and Mystus thrive here.

A photo that spans 50 years of mahseer conservation. 

Today's mahseer conservationists in the Cauvery meet India's celebrated mahseer breeder. WASI guards at the Shivanasamudram Fish Sanctuary were ecstatic to meet Dr. Shashank Ogale from the TATA group, who has worked tirelessly to develop protocols to breed mahseer in the Picturesque Lonavala Mahseer Hatchery in Pune.

Deccan Mahseer (Tor khudree) from India. Possibly Western Ghats. I've been searching for these for a long time & finally I've imported them myself.

#tor #torkhudree #deccanmahseer #mahseer #indianfish #fish #freshwaterfish #cyprinidae #ikan #kelah #malaysia #aquariumfish #fishtank #fishphotography #beautifulfish #rarefish

Our search for the humpback mahseer has been a challenge, but one that has been made truly successful by several volunteers braving very harsh conditions. 

This year, we are diving right into the young-of-year (YOY), to see what secrets these little torpedoes have to reveal about natural recruitment among the mahseer of the Cauvery. 

Stay tuned for updates on our YOY surveys.

രുചി ഇഷ്ടമല്ലെന്ന പേരിൽ ബാസ മീൻ മലയാളികൾ കഴിക്കാറില്ല. പക്ഷെ കാറ്ററിങ് ടീം തന്ത്രപരമായി ഇവന്റുകളിൽ ബാസ മീൻ ഉൾപ്പെടുത്താറുണ്ട്. ഇവന്റുകൾക്ക് ഊണിനൊപ്പം സൗജന്യമായി മീൻ ഡിഷ് കൂടെ ഉൾപ്പെടുത്താം എന്ന് പറയുന്നുണ്ടെങ്കിൽ അത് ബാസ മീൻ ആയിരിക്കും. കേന്ദ്ര സമുദ്ര മത്സ്യ ഗവേഷണ സ്ഥാപനം ഡയറക്ടർ ഡോ. ഗ്രിൻസൺ ജോർജ്ജ് സംസാരിക്കുന്നു.

Some Topwater Action from the Kannur Region post monsoon!!!
Our guide @angler_sharon landing a chunky Malabar mahseer on 
@bonefishingworld Hoverjet 130!!
Fishing in the Kannur region is heaven for topwater enthusiasts the way these fish hit the bait on flowing river will rise your adrenaline rush into max!!!

Other than mahseer there are more species you can unlock in kannur for ex. Malabar Snake...

Just imagine the pre monsoon in the southern part of india, melody of the flowing river and exotic birds,clear sky and greenery around and you're fishing for this beautiful yet stronger fish with us!!!
A moment that will be precious for your entire life!!!
We can't explain it in word's just visit and experience yourself and we guarantee you'll be coming back to this place again and again!!!

Wildlife Association of South India, empowered legends like Renuka for 50+ years, to patrol the banks of the Cauvery and ensure protection of the Humpback Mahseer from illegal dynamiting...

Last week, we bid farewell to a true legend of the Cauvery. Renuka, from Dassanadhoddi village, was a cornerstone of the mahseer angling community. He joined the Cauvery fishing camps in the early 2000s and dedicated the next decade to honing his craft as a skilled angling guide. Beyond that, he spent another ten years safeguarding the river and the forests of the Cauvery Wildlife Sanctuary.

In memory of a humpback mahseer that would remain the largest known for 64 years, 11months and 9 days. 

On the 22nd of March 1946, J deWet van Ingen of Mysore took a trip to the upper regions of the Kabini. Equipped with a coracle (local boat), rod-line and 'atta' for bait, he returned to his camp with a mahseer weighing in at 54.43 kilograms. An all India record.

The Noah's ark from Indian Mythology

The legend of ‘Matsya' is a fascinating parable from Indian mythology. Some people believe that the fish in the story can be equated to the Mahseer. It is thought that the name 'Mahseer' is derived from Matsya (meaning fish); one of 10 incarnations of lord Vishnu.

Mahseer scales are fascinating. This one, illuminated by a flashlight offers a fantastic photograph. The scales are large and have a ton of detail.

Today's Cauvery river with her various stressors can no longer host such magnificent aquatic fauna.
